---
title: Experience
---

<div class="page-shell page-shell-inner">
  <section class="section reveal">
    <div class="section-heading">
      <p class="eyebrow">Experience</p>
      <h2>Career path</h2>
    </div>
    <div class="timeline">
      <article class="timeline-item">
        <div class="timeline-marker"></div>
        <div class="timeline-content">
          <p class="timeline-period">2026-present</p>
          <h3>Senior Director, Biostatistics</h3>
          <p class="timeline-org">Datavant (formerly Aetion)</p>
          <p>
            I lead the biostatistics and statistical programming functions within Datavant's
            Science organization. My work focuses on building best practices for regulatory-grade
            real-world evidence studies, developing internal tools like R packages and Shiny
            applications, and partnering with product teams on future opportunities, including
            thoughtful uses of AI. It is a role that combines scientific quality, team leadership,
            and operational execution.
          </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article class="timeline-item">
        <div class="timeline-marker"></div>
        <div class="timeline-content">
          <p class="timeline-period">Recent</p>
          <h3>Biostatistics leadership in industry</h3>
          <p class="timeline-org">Tempus AI and 23andMe</p>
          <p>
            Before Datavant, I held biostatistics roles at Tempus AI and 23andMe. Those
            experiences expanded my perspective on analytics in industry settings and deepened my
            interest in building practical, high-impact systems at the interface of data science,
            health research, and product development.
          </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article class="timeline-item">
        <div class="timeline-marker"></div>
        <div class="timeline-content">
          <p class="timeline-period">Earlier career</p>
          <h3>Faculty and research leadership at the University of Kentucky</h3>
          <p class="timeline-org">Assistant Professor and Biostatistician</p>
          <p>
            Earlier in my career, I spent years at the University of Kentucky, first as a
            biostatistician and later as an Assistant Professor. That work gave me a strong
            foundation in study design, collaborative research, and the practical realities of
            supporting scientific teams across a wide range of clinical and translational projects.
          </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article class="timeline-item">
        <div class="timeline-marker"></div>
        <div class="timeline-content">
          <p class="timeline-period">Training</p>
          <h3>PhD in Biostatistics</h3>
          <p class="timeline-org">University of Kentucky</p>
          <p>
            My doctoral training centered on dementia, genetics, observational analysis, and
            translational health science. It shaped the statistical rigor that still anchors how I
            approach evidence generation today.
          </p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </section>
</div>
